title: Sundays Are Fun as a Caricature Artist
excerpt: This past Sunday I drew caricatures at Sam Houston Race Park’s Sunday Fundays — and despite a threatening sky, it turned out to be a great afternoon of live event caricatures in Houston.
imageAlt: Steven Simon drawing caricatures at Sam Houston Race Park Sunday Fundays
content:
This past Sunday I had the pleasure of drawing caricatures at Sam Houston Race Park’s Sunday Fundays — one of my favorite kinds of gigs. We sprang forward an hour that morning, the sky looked like it had other plans, but the weather held and the crowd showed up ready to have a good time. I drew so many people I honestly lost count, which is always a good sign.

One thing I love about events like Sunday Fundays is how accessible they are. If you’ve ever priced out entertainment at something like RodeoHouston, you know the cost adds up fast. Sunday Fundays at Sam Houston Race Park offers a fun, family-friendly afternoon in Houston without that price tag — and a live caricature booth fits right into that vibe. Guests of all ages lined up, laughed at their drawings, and walked away with something to hang on the fridge. That’s exactly why I got into this.

Sam Houston Race Park is out in the Spring, Texas area — a bit of a trek from where I’m based near Kingwood, but absolutely worth the drive. If you’re planning an outdoor event, a festival, or a recurring weekly event anywhere in the greater Houston area and want a caricature artist who can keep up with a crowd, I’d love to be part of it. Live caricatures are one of the best ways to add memorable, personal entertainment to any event — rain clouds and all.

title: Drawing Caricatures on the Road: Buda, Burton, and a Wedding in Texas
excerpt: From Buda’s Birthday Bash near Austin to the Cotton Gin Festival in Burton and a wild wedding reception, April has been a road trip for the sketchbooks — and I wouldn’t have it any other way.
imageAlt: Steven Simon drawing caricatures at festivals and events across Texas
content:
April has been one of those months that reminds me why I love being a traveling caricature artist in Texas. Three events, three different cities, and somewhere in between — a really good burger and a slice of wedding cake.

Buda’s Birthday Bash — Austin, Texas

We kicked things off at Buda’s Birthday Bash, a fantastic community festival just south of Austin. The Austin area always brings out an incredibly fun, artsy, expressive crowd — exactly the kind of faces a caricature artist loves to draw. Everyone leans into it. Before the event we stopped into a P. Terry’s for lunch, and if you’ve never been, you’re missing out — best burger stop in Texas as far as I’m concerned. For dinner we ended the night at Aba, which did not disappoint. Austin has a way of making everything feel like an experience. I’ll be honest — I think about moving there someday.

The Cotton Gin Festival — Burton, Texas

Next up was the Cotton Gin Festival in Burton, Texas, out in Washington County between Houston and Austin. The weather had other plans — rainy, windy, and surprisingly cold for April. But the people who showed up were troopers, and the drawings were some of my most exaggerated work of the month. There’s something about cold weather that makes people loosen up and laugh a little harder. Two of my autographed caricatures found new homes that day too — a Craig Biggio and a Marcel Reed both sold on the spot. If you’re looking to pick up a one-of-a-kind autographed piece, you can browse my full collection at stevensimon.art/shop.

A Wedding Reception — Wild and Wonderful

Then came the wedding. I won’t say it was my most relaxed evening — it was busy. Nonstop from the moment I set up. Wedding guests are some of the most fun people to draw because everyone’s already in a celebratory mood, and the exaggeration requests get creative fast. My Paul Wall and Gene Simmons autographs on display got some serious head turns from guests, which is always a good sign. We even managed to sneak a slice of cake before the night was over. Worth every bite.

Coming Up: Seguin & the San Antonio Riverwalk

Next week I’m headed to Seguin, Texas for the Earth Day festival, and then on to the San Antonio Riverwalk. Central Texas keeps delivering some of the most diverse, expressive faces I’ve ever drawn — and that’s really the heart of why I do this. Every person is different. Every face tells a story. I genuinely believe caricature is part of my purpose — that God places me at the right events, in front of the right people, at the right time. A caricature isn’t just a funny drawing. It’s a little stamp that says: you are unique, and that is worth celebrating.

If you’re planning a festival, wedding, corporate event, or community gathering anywhere in Central or South Texas — Austin, San Antonio, Seguin, Buda, or beyond — I’d love to be there. Book now and let’s make it happen.

title: May in Motion: College Station, Brenham, Houston, and Austin
excerpt: May has been an adventure — from Northgate in College Station to Maifest in Brenham, a 90's night at the Houston Museum of Natural Science, and late-night drawings on Rainey Street in Austin.
imageAlt: Steven Simon drawing caricatures at events across Texas in May
content:
May has been an adventure so far — and we're not even done yet.

Back to Northgate — College Station, Texas

It started with a trip to College Station to draw caricatures at Northgate. I'm a Texas A&M alumnus (Class of 2014, Bachelor's in Visualization), so that stretch of bars and venues near campus genuinely feels like home. I wanted to get at least one night there before the students headed back for the summer — and we made the most of it. We caught Mass at St. Mary's, tried Juanita's for the first time, and the Savannah Bananas were in town that day, so there were plenty of kids in the mix to draw too.

Anakin's 1st Birthday

From there I headed to a first birthday party — Anakin's first birthday, to be exact. Private parties are some of my favorite gigs because of how personal they feel. I also sold my Paul Wall autographed caricature that day, which was a nice bonus.

Maifest — Brenham, Texas

Next was Maifest in Brenham. I had planned to stay the full day, but a heavy ten-minute downpour had other ideas. I pack light by design — just an easel, chairs, a sign, and some autographs on display — so when the weather turns, I can move fast. No tent to wrestle with, no trailer to unhitch. That setup also makes me easy to fit into indoor parties and home events, which I love. So I packed up, shook off the rain, and drove back up to College Station for one last night at Northgate before the semester wrapped.

That night turned into something I'll remember for a while. A woman came up and handed me a Bible — completely unprompted — and ended up walking away with my Dora the Explorer / Kathleen Herles autographed caricature. It was one of those moments where you just know you're in exactly the right place. I genuinely believe God puts me at the right events, in front of the right people, at the right time — and that night was a clear reminder of it.

Houston Museum of Natural Science — Sugar Land 90's Night

The next week brought one of the more unique settings I've drawn in: the Houston Museum of Natural Science in Sugar Land for their 90's Night. Dinosaurs in the background, cool hats and sunglasses, plaid shirts everywhere — it was a scene. I still have the Backstreet Boys rattling around in my head from that night. No complaints.

Sweet Home Fireman's Fest — Sweet Home, Texas

The very next day I was at the Sweet Home Fireman's Fest — my first time in Sweet Home, Texas. It was a hot one, but the kids I drew had an absolute blast. Lots of smiles, American flags flying, and the Shiner Hobo Band playing live — I even drew a caricature of the entire band, which was a first for this month. Since Sweet Home put me closer to Austin than Houston, I kept driving west, scouted out some future drawing locations around the city, and ended the evening on Rainey Street drawing a couple of faces near the bars. I grabbed Terry Black's BBQ while I was there. No regrets.

May in Motion — Still to Come

May in Motion isn't over yet. I've got a Project Graduation for Barbers Hill coming up — which means I'll be up all night for that one — and then Sam Houston Race Park's Character Derby Day to close out the month. More faces, more road, more Texas.

If you're planning a summer event — festivals, graduations, birthday parties, corporate events — anywhere across Texas, I'd love to be there. Book now and let's make it happen.

title: June in Full Swing: Houston Dinosaurs, Steampunk Festivals, Rodeos, and Rainy-Day Caricatures
excerpt: June brought T. rex Week at the Houston Museum of Natural Science, the Galveston Steampunk Festival, the Crosby Fair and Rodeo, Mixers & Elixirs 80's Night, and even a birthday party in the rain.
imageAlt: Steven Simon drawing caricatures at Houston Museum of Natural Science and events across Texas in June
keywords: Houston Museum of Natural Science events
content:
June has been one of those months where it feels like I’ve been everywhere at once. Dinosaurs, steampunk costumes, muddy rodeo grounds, neon-colored museum parties, and even a birthday party in the rain — every week brought something completely different.

T. rex Week at the Houston Museum of Natural Science

The month started at the Houston Museum of Natural Science during T. rex Week. I spent Tuesday drawing caricatures inside one of Houston’s most iconic museums, and it was packed with dinosaur fans of all ages. Later that week, museum members were treated to a members-only event where complimentary caricatures were included as part of the experience. If you're a Houston family looking for ways to get more value from a museum membership, events like these are hard to beat.

Galveston Steampunk Festival and a Stop at Gaido's

Next came the Galveston Steampunk Festival, which has become one of our favorite annual traditions. This was our third year attending, and the creativity never disappoints. Steampunk events are a caricature artist’s dream — elaborate costumes, unique hats, goggles, and plenty of larger-than-life personalities to draw.

Before heading inland, we made a stop at Gaido's Seafood Restaurant. If you're ever visiting Galveston, it’s one of those classic Texas Gulf Coast restaurants that belongs on the must-visit list.

Crosby Fair and Rodeo — A Trip Back Home

That same day we made our way to the Crosby Fair and Rodeo. The grounds were muddy from recent rain, but that didn’t slow anyone down. We drew caricatures for local families, teenagers, couples, and plenty of kids throughout the event.

Crosby holds a special place for me because it’s where I grew up. Walking through the fairgrounds always brings back memories, but this year I noticed something different: I hardly recognized anyone. That’s not a bad thing. It simply shows how much Crosby continues to grow.

Mixers & Elixirs — 80's Night

The following week brought me back to the Houston Museum of Natural Science for their popular Mixers & Elixirs series. This month’s theme was 80’s Night, and the crowd fully committed. Bright neon colors, oversized hair, retro outfits, and nonstop energy made for one of the busiest nights I’ve had this year.

If you're looking for a unique Houston date night or a fun pre-game stop before heading out downtown, the museum’s evening events are worth checking out. The next Mixers & Elixirs event is Masquerade Night on July 10, and I expect the costumes will be incredible.

Rain? No Problem.

Today I wrapped up a birthday party for a six-year-old, and Mother Nature decided to test us a little. Rain showers rolled through, but the party kept going. Kids still swam, families still celebrated, and I kept drawing. A few raindrops landed on the paper here and there, but nothing stopped the fun.

One thing I’ve learned after years of drawing caricatures across Texas is that weather doesn’t have to ruin an event. Whether it’s a birthday party, festival, company picnic, or community gathering, there are usually ways to make it work. Rain or shine, I’m ready to draw.

Looking Ahead to the Fourth of July

Summer is officially here, and Fourth of July season is already starting. Next week I’ll be heading to Corsicana, Texas for Independence Day celebrations and drawing caricatures for another great crowd.

If you're planning a birthday party, festival, museum event, corporate gathering, rodeo, or Fourth of July celebration anywhere in Texas, I’d love to be part of it. Book now and let’s make it happen.
